# Figure description: Figure 7.png

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
Geophysical map (airborne geophysical survey image, likely radiometric or electromagnetic data rendered as a false-colour image, combined with a structural interpretation overlay). The base image appears to be a Total Magnetic Intensity (TMI) or similar potential-field dataset rendered with hillshade/colour-coding to highlight lithological and structural contrasts. It functions as a mineral prospectivity/target generation map.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers an area in the **Murchison region of Western Australia**, centred on and around the town of **Coe** (labelled in the lower-right portion of the map). The coverage spans approximately **15–20 km north–south and 12–15 km east–west**, based on the scale bar showing 0–5 km. The area lies within the **Murchison Goldfield**, a well-known Archaean greenstone belt within the Yilgarn Craton. The map displays tenement boundaries (black solid polygons), interpreted structural lineaments, and gold mineral occurrence data overlaid on the geophysical base image.

---

## Key Content

**Base Geophysical Image:**
The background is a false-colour geophysical image (consistent with TMI or a derivative such as analytic signal or first vertical derivative), rendered in a **rainbow/spectral colour palette** (blues, purples, greens, yellows, reds). The colour banding reflects variations in magnetic susceptibility or radiometric response of underlying lithologies. Distinctive **linear, sub-parallel colour banding** trending approximately **NNW–SSE** dominates the central and western portions of the map, consistent with folded or steeply dipping greenstone belt stratigraphy. The eastern portion shows a more diffuse, warmer-coloured (red, orange, yellow) signature suggesting different lithology or structural character, potentially granitoid rocks or a magnetic low zone near Coe.

**Structural Lineaments:**
Two sets of interpreted structural lineaments are shown:
- **Black dashed lines** — labelled as `2_5m_interpstrucl15` (likely 1:250,000-scale interpreted structures), representing interpreted faults or shear zones trending broadly NNW–SSE, running through the central part of the map.
- **White dashed lines** — labelled as `500k_interp structure` (1:500,000-scale interpreted structures), representing regional-scale structural features. These are more broadly spaced and also trend NNW–SSE, with at least one curving feature visible in the northeast.

**Tenement Boundaries:**
Multiple **solid black polygons** (irregular rectangles and polygons) define exploration tenement boundaries covering the central greenstone corridor. These tenements are clustered along the geophysically active NNW-trending corridor, indicating where the reporting company holds ground.

**Red Ellipses (Target Areas):**
Five prominent **red ellipses** are annotated on the map, marking interpreted high-priority exploration targets. These are positioned:
- Two ellipses in the **upper-central** area (northeast of the main tenement cluster), oriented NNW, located within or adjacent to prominent structural intersections.
- One ellipse in the **mid-central** area, elongated NNW.
- Two ellipses in the **lower-central/southern** area, elongated NNW, overlapping the southern tenement blocks.
All red ellipses are positioned along or adjacent to the interpreted structural lineaments (black dashed lines) and within the tenement boundaries, suggesting they represent drill targets or zones of geophysical/structural interest.

**Gold Mineral Occurrences:**
**Yellow/orange diamond symbols** (◇) are scattered across the entire map area, representing known **gold mineral occurrences** from the WAMEX/GSWA mineral occurrence database. These are densely concentrated:
- Along the NNW-trending structural corridor in the centre of the map.
- Around and southeast of Coe town (dense cluster).
- Along the western margin of the map.
- Scattered throughout the eastern half of the map.
A small number of **green/white diamond symbols** are also present (possibly a different mineral occurrence type or a subset of gold occurrences with different confidence/classification).

**Colour Patterns of Note:**
- A prominent **warm-coloured (red/orange/brown) elongated anomaly** runs roughly N–S through the centre-right of the map, near Coe, possibly indicating a magnetic high or radiometric anomaly associated with a significant lithological contact or intrusive body.
- The **blue-purple linear bands** in the west likely represent mafic/ultramafic greenstones with higher magnetic susceptibility or lower radiometric response.

## Research Value

This figure synthesises **geophysical data, regional structural interpretation, tenement holdings, and historical gold occurrence data** to visually communicate the mineral prospectivity of a corridor within the Murchison Goldfield near Coe, WA. The red ellipses specifically communicate the company's interpreted **drill target zones**, generated from the coincidence of favourable geophysical signatures, structural controls (interpreted faults/shear zones), and proximity to known gold occurrences — the classic Archaean lode gold targeting criteria. A geologist or AI research assistant would use this figure to understand **where the reporting company believes gold mineralisation is most likely to occur**, how these targets relate to the regional structural architecture of the greenstone belt, and how the tenement package is spatially positioned relative to historical gold workings and geophysical anomalies.
